'''Andrew Hick''' (born {{birth date|1971|2|11|df=y}}) is an Australian former [[rugby league]] footballer who played professionally in [[England]] and Australia.

==Playing career==
Hick played for the [[Engadine Dragons]] before making his début for the local [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] in the [[NSWRL Premiership]] in 1991. He played in 44 matches for the Sharks over the next five years before transferring to the [[Western Suburbs Magpies]] in 1996. In 1997 he switched to [[Super League (Australasia)|Super League]], joining the new [[Adelaide Rams]] franchise.<ref name=rlp/>

In 1999 Hick moved to England, spending one season each with the new [[Gateshead Thunder (1999)|Gateshead Thunder]] club in 1999 and [[Hull FC]] in 2000. He was one of several former Sharks players who played for the Thunder.<ref>[https://archive.today/20130125115112/http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-4982571.html Lee brings the beach life to Thunder road] ''The Independent'', 18 April 1999</ref> Hick retired at the end of the 2000 season.<ref>[https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/2999879/Super-League-Team-by-team-guide.html Super League Team-by-team guide] ''The Telegraph'', 1 March 2001</ref>
